title: Tridactyle anthomaniaca
taxonomy: Tridactyle anthomaniaca is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. Tridactyle anthomaniaca is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Orchideae. At the genus level, it is classified as Tridactyle, a genus of flowering plants known for their unique growth habits. The classification of Tridactyle anthomaniaca reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchids.

intro: Tridactyle anthomaniaca, commonly known as the 'Ladies' Tresses', is a terrestrial orchid species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of various infections, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Tridactyle anthomaniaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tridactyle anthomaniaca include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to harness its therapeutic potential.
